title: pleiogynium timoriense
intro: Pleiogynium timoriense, also known as the Black Bean Tree, is a deciduous tree native to the tropical forests of Australia and New Guinea. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its seeds, which contain a toxic compound called timoriin. The seeds of the Black Bean Tree have been used in traditional medicine to treat various health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and gastrointestinal problems. The seeds can be prepared into herbal preparations such as infusions, decoctions, or extracts, which can be used to alleviate symptoms and promote overall well-being.
